title: One-time things don't matter — habits inventory
html:
TL;DR

  • Aphoristic open: "One time things don't really matter what matter in the end is the habits that one has/constructs."
  • Bad-habits column: lying about himself, biting nails, relying on others for action (specifically Owen for gym, Yermy for plans).
  • "Maybe bad habit (feel like I need to always improve)" — flagged as ambiguous.
  • Good-habits column: "Can say no more than most", determination is hard to stop, family-as-drive, awareness that time at osu / in life is limited.
  • Specimen of habit inventory as a recurring self-evaluation form.

Key quotes

"One time things don't really matter what matter in the end is the habits that one has/constructs"

"*maybe bad habit (feel like I need to always improve"

"Aware that our time is limited here at OSU and in life"

Why it matters in the graph

  • "Owen / gym" — Owen is a recurring name (also in 2025 05 09 initiation to chelympics beer-ball league and 2025 05 11 owen hospital count). The Owen-as-gym-partner dependency is documented here in 2022.
  • "Yermy" = emilio's nickname (his brother). The plans-with-Yermy dependency reads as a closeness-of-bond thing, not actually a flaw.
  • "Aware our time is limited" — proto-articulation of legacy question. He's 19/20 here.

Open questions

  • "Always need to improve" — did he ever resolve whether this is bad? (Looks like by 2026 he's leaning into it.)
  • Did the gym-with-Owen dependency get fixed?

Wikilinks

osu · corvallis or · emilio · legacy question · fear of not doing
